edges.description: Loss of sialidase prevents desialylation, so sialylated substrates accumulate from before birth.

meta.association: Biallelic NEU1 variants abolishing lysosomal neuraminidase-1 (severe alleles)

evidence.reference_title: Sialidosis: A Review of Morphology and Molecular Biology of a Rare Pediatric Disorder.
evidence.snippet:
An association exists between the impact of the individual mutations and the
severity of clinical presentation of sialidosis.

nodes.description: Biallelic NEU1 variants abolish lysosomal neuraminidase-1 (alpha-N-acetyl neuraminidase). The profound deficiency of the congenital form prevents removal of terminal sialic acid from sialoglycoconjugates from before birth.

evidence.reference_title: Sialidosis: A Review of Morphology and Molecular Biology of a Rare Pediatric Disorder.
evidence.snippet:
caused by α-N-acetyl neuraminidase deficiency resulting from a mutation in
the neuraminidase gene (NEU1)

evidence.reference_title: Pathogenesis, Emerging therapeutic targets and Treatment in Sialidosis.
evidence.snippet: caused by mutations in the NEU1 gene, encoding the lysosomal sialidase NEU1

nodes.description: Without sialidase activity, sialylated oligosaccharides and glycopeptides accumulate markedly in lysosomes (and are excreted in urine) from prenatal life, producing severe multisystem storage disease with hydrops.

evidence.reference_title: Sialidosis: A Review of Morphology and Molecular Biology of a Rare Pediatric Disorder.
evidence.snippet: This genetic alteration leads to abnormal intracellular accumulation as well as urinary excretion of
